2604.13833.title: Robust Reward Modeling for Large Language Models via Causal Decomposition
2604.13833.paper_summary: Reward models are central to aligning large language models, yet they often overfit to spurious cues such as response length and overly agreeable tone. Most prior work weakens these cues directly by penalizing or controlling specific artifacts, but it does not explicitly encourage the model to ground preferences in the prompt's intent. We learn a decoder that maps a candidate answer to the latent intent embedding of the input. The reconstruction error is used as a signal to regularize the reward model training. We provide theoretical evidence that this signal emphasizes prompt-dependent information while suppressing prompt-independent shortcuts. Across math, helpfulness, and safety benchmarks, the decoder selects shorter and less sycophantic candidates with 0.877 accuracy. Incorporating this signal into RM training in Gemma-2-2B-it and Gemma-2-9B-it increases RewardBench accuracy from 0.832 to 0.868. For Best-of-N selection, our framework increases length-controlled win rates while producing shorter outputs, and remains robust to lengthening and mild off-topic drift in controlled rewrite tests.
2604.13833.paper_summary_zh: 獎勵模型對於對齊大型語言模型至關重要，但它們經常過度擬合於虛假的線索，例如回應長度和過於迎合的語氣。大多數先前的工作通過懲罰或控制特定的工件來直接削弱這些線索，但並未明確鼓勵模型將偏好根植於提示的意圖中。我們學習了一個解碼器，將候選答案映射到輸入的潛在意圖嵌入。重建誤差被用作正則化獎勵模型訓練的信號。我們提供理論證據表明，這個信號強調了依賴提示的信息，同時抑制了獨立於提示的捷徑。在數學、有用性和安全性基準中，解碼器以 0.877 的準確率選擇較短且不那麼拍馬屁的候選者。將這個信號納入 Gemma-2-2B-it 和 Gemma-2-9B-it 的 RM 訓練中，將 RewardBench 的準確率從 0.832 提高到 0.868。對於 Best-of-N 選擇，我們的框架在產生較短輸出的同時提高了長度控制的勝率，並在受控重寫測試中對於延長和輕微的偏題漂移保持穩健。
2604.13833.author: Yunsheng Lu et.al.
2604.13833.authors: Yunsheng Lu, Zijiang Yang, Licheng Pan, Zhixuan Chu
